info.title: Compliance Controls API
info.description:
You can export the OpenAPI specs in the [Cortex Stoplight instance](https://cortex-panw.stoplight.io/docs/cortex-xsiam-3-x/3kdu9xjq1aw21-compliance-controls-api). 

API for managing compliance controls, rules and retrieving compliance results.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

post.description:
An assessment profile uses a standard to run scans on an asset group to check whether the assets adhere to the standard.

Retrieve compliance assessment profiles with optional filtering, sorting and pagination.
- The response is concatenated using AND condition (OR is not supported)
- The maximum result set size is >100
- Offset is the zero-based number of assessment profiles from the start of the result set

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

An assessment profile uses a standard to run scans on an asset group to check whether the assets adhere to the standard.

Get details of the assessment profile specified by its ID.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

Create a custom compliance assessment profile that is tailored to your own business needs and organizational policies.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

report_type.description:
Assessment profile repor type. Valid values include:
- `ALL`
- `PDF`
- `CSV`
- `NONE`

success.description: Whether the assessment profile creation succeeded

Edit an existing compliance custom assessment profile.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

Delete an assessment profile specified by its ID.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

success.description: Whether the assessment profile deletion succeeded

post.description:
Retrieve assessment profile results with optional filtering based on the last successful evaluation. Sorting will not take effect even if it is specified in the payload.

**Filtering Support:**
- Field (`labels`): `contains`, `not_contains`

**Note:** This API does not support sorting or pagination.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

Compliance controls are measures related to the standard that ensure compliance and mitigate risks. Controls are built from one or more rules, the specific checks that run on an asset. Controls can be grouped into categories, for example RBAC and Pod security.

Retrieve compliance control details with optional filtering, sorting and pagination.
- The response is concatenated using AND condition (OR is not supported)
- The maximum result set size is >100
- Offset is the zero-based number of assessment profiles from the start of the result set

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

keyword.description: Sort in ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`) order

search_from.description:
Integer representing the starting offset within the query result set from which you want compliance controls returned.
Compliance controls are returned as a zero-based list. Any compliance control indexed less than this value is not returned in the final result set and defaults to zero.

search_to.description:
Integer representing the end offset within the result set after which you do not want compliance controls returned.
Compliance controls in the compliance control list that are indexed higher than this value are not returned in the final results set. Defaults to >100, which returns all compliance controls to the end of the list.

result_count.description: Number of controls matching filters + paging

post.description:
Retrieve archived compliance assessment reports with optional filtering, sorting and pagination.
- The response is concatenated using AND condition (OR is not supported)
- The maximum result set size is >100
- Offset is the zero-based number of assessment profiles from the start of the result set

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

filters.description: Optional filters to apply to the reports

total_count.description: Total number of reports in the archive

result_count.description: Number of reports returned in this response (filter + paging)

filter_count.description: Number of reports matching the filter criteria

post.description:
Retrieve compliance standards with optional filtering, sorting and pagination.
- The response is concatenated using AND condition (OR is not supported)
- The maximum result set size is >100
- Offset is the zero-based number of assessment profiles from the start of the result set

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

result_count.description: Number of standards matching the filter criteria + paging

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

controls_ids.description: List of control IDs to associate with this standard. Only custom controls can be included.

post.description:
Delete a the compliance standard specified by its ID.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security or Cortex Cloud Posture Management

Retrieve compliance assets with required assessment profile revision and last evaluation time, with optional filtering, sorting and pagination.

**Filtering Support:**
- **String fields** (`asset_name`, `rule_name`, `control`): `eq`, `neq`, `contains`, `not_contains`
- **Status fields** (`asset_type`, `status`, `severity`, `rule_type`): `eq`, `neq`
- Status valid values: [`FAILED`, `PASSED`, `NOT_ASSESSED`]
- Severity valid values: [`low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`]

**Sorting Support:**
- Available fields: `asset_name`, `asset_type`, `status`, `rule_name`, `control`, `severity`
- Sort orders: `asc`, `desc`
- Default: `asset_name` in ascending order

**Pagination:** (max number of elements is 100)

search_from.description:
Integer representing the starting offset within the query result set from which you want assessment profiles returned.
Assessment profiles are returned as a zero-based list. Any assessment profile indexed less than this value is not returned in the final result set and defaults to zero.

search_to.description:
Integer representing the end offset within the result set after which you do not want assessment profiles returned.
Assessment profiles in the assessment profile list that are indexed higher than this value are not returned in the final results set. Defaults to >100, which returns all assessment profiles to the end of the list.

oneOf.description:
Whether the assessment profile is enabled. Valid values are:
- `yes`
- `no`
Whether the assessment profile is enabled

enabled.description: Enable or disable the assessment profile

report_targets.description: List of email addresses for reports

report_type.description:
Type of reporting. Valid values include:
- `ALL`
- `PDF`
- `CSV`
- `NONE`

evaluation_frequency.description: Cron expression for assessment report evaluation frequency

field.description:
Identifies the assessment profile field the filter should match. Filters are based on the following keywords:
- `name`: Assessment profile name
- `standard_name` : Standard name
- `description`: Assessment profile description
- `asset_group_name`: Asset Group name
- `created_by`: Creator of the assessment profile
- `modified_by`: Modifier of the assessment profile
- `id`: Assessment profile ID
- `report_type`: Report type
- `standard_id`: Standard ID
- `creation_time`: Assessment profile's creation time
- `modification_time`: Assessment profile's modification time
- `enabled`: Whether the assessment profile is enabled
- `asset_group_id`: Asset Group ID

operator.description:
Identifies the comparison operator you want to use for this filter. Valid keywords are:

`eq` / `neq` / `contains` / `not_contains`

- `name`, `standard_name`, `description`, `asset_group_name`, `created_by`, `modified_by`, `id`, `report_type`, `standard_id`: String

`gte`, `lte`, `range`, `relative_timestamp`
- `creation_time`, `modification_time`: Integer in timestamp epoch milliseconds

`in`
- `enabled`: valid values are `yes` or `no`

`eq` / `neq`
- `asset_group_id`: String

oneOf.description: When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type string: `name`, `standard_name`, `description`, `asset_group_name`, `created_by`, `modified_by`, `id`, `report_type`, `standard_id`, `asset_group_id`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type integer: `creation_time`, `modification_time`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type enum: `enabled`

value.description: Value that this filter must match. The contents of this field will differ depending on the assessment profile `field` that you specified for this filter

keyword.description: Sort in ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`) order

enabled.description: Whether the assessment profile is enabled

result_count.description: Number of records matching the filter + paging

total_count.description: Total number of assessment results available

result_count.description: Number of assessment results matching the applied filters

field.description:
Identifies the compliance control field the filter should match. Filters are based on the following keywords:
- `name`: Compliance control name
- `category` : Compliance control category
- `subcategory`: Compliance control subcategory
- `description`: Compliance control description
- `created_by`: Creator of the compliance control
- `modified_by`: Modifier of the compliance control
- `id`: Compliance control ID
- `creation_time`: Compliance control's creation time
- `modification_time`: Compliance control's modification time
- `is_custom`: Whether the compliance control is custom

operator.description:
Identifies the comparison operator you want to use for this filter. Valid keywords are:

`eq` / `neq` / `contains` / `not_contains`: 
- `name`, `category`, `subcategory`, `created_by`, `modified_by`, `id`, `description`: String

`gte`, `lte`, `range`, `relative_timestamp`: 
- `creation_time`, `modification_time`: Integer in timestamp epoch milliseconds

`in`:
- `is_custom`: valid values are `yes` or `no`

oneOf.description: When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type string: `name`, `category`, `subcategory`, `created_by`, `modified_by`, `id`, `description`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type integer: `creation_time`, `modification_time`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type enum: `is_custom`

field.description:
Identifies the compliance report field the filter should match. Filters are based on the following keywords:
- `standard_name`: Standard name
- `assessment_profile_name`: Assessment profile name
- `asset_group_name`: Asset Group name
- `score`: Compliance score
- `evaluation_time`: Evaluation time

operator.description:
Identifies the comparison operator you want to use for this filter. Valid keywords are:

`eq` / `neq` / `contains` / `not_contains`: 
- `standard_name`, `assessment_profile_name`, `asset_group_name`: String

`gte` / `lte` / `range` / `relative_timestamp`: 
- `evaluation_time``: Integer in timestamp epoch milliseconds

`gte` / `lte` / `eq` / `neq`:
- `score`: Integer

oneOf.description: When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type string: `standard_name`, `assessment_profile_name`, `asset_group_name`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type integer: `evaluation_time`, `score`

value.description: Value that this filter must match. The contents of this field will differ depending on the assessment profile `field` that you specified for this filter.

field.description:
Identifies the compliance standard field the filter should match. Filters are based on the following keywords:
- `name`: Compliance standard name
- `id`: Compliance standard ID
- `publisher`: Compliance standard publisher
- `created_by`: User who created the compliance standard
- `insertion_time`: When the compliance standard was created
- `is_custom`: Whether the compliance standard is custom
- `labels`: Compliance standard labels

operator.description:
Identifies the comparison operator you want to use for this filter. Valid keywords are:

`eq` / `neq` / `contains` / `not_contains`: 
- `name`, `id`, `publisher`, `created_by`: String

`gte` / `lte` / `range` / `relative_timestamp`: 
- `insertion_time``: Integer in timestamp epoch milliseconds

`in`:
- `is_custom`: Enum with possible values of `yes` or `no`

`contains` / `not_contains`:
- `labels`: Array of strings

oneOf.description: When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type string: `name`, `id`, `publisher`, `created_by`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type integer: `insertion_time`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is type enum: `is_custom`
When the `field` value is one of the following, the `value` is an array of strings: `labels`

value.description: Value that this filter must match. The contents of this field will differ depending on the assessment profile `field` that you specified for this filter.

reply.err_msg: Invalid score value. Score must be between 0 and 100

Invalid sort field. Allowed fields: standard_name, assessment_profile_name, asset_group_name, score, evaluation_time
Invalid filter field 'invalid_field'. Allowed fields: standard_name, assessment_profile_name, asset_group_name, score, evaluation_time
Invalid filter field. Allowed fields: name, category, subcategory, is_custom, creation_time, created_by, modified_by, modification_time, id, description
Invalid operator 'range' for field 'name'. Allowed operators: eq, neq, contains, not_contains
Invalid value for is_custom field. Allowed values: yes, no
